# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf
# Category: command_injection

namespace :backup do
    desc "Generate a snapshot of your database" # Explanation in markdown will go here if needed, unless overridden by task arguments or options hash below (like -s => [default_value]) 
  
	task generate:[options[:name] || 'db'] , :environment do |t|                     
      puts "Backing up database #{ENV['RAILS_ENV']} to tarball at: '#{[backupfile].absolute}'"                   
      `tar czf /path/to/your-dir.tar.gz db --exclude=node_modules/* -b 192.168.0.* | ssh user@host 'cat > /remote/destination' `     # Explanation in markdown will go here if needed, unless overridden by task arguments or options hash below (like :name => [default_value])
	end  		 	 	   			   									     	       							           						       end                                                  `ruby -e "$SAFE = 3" # Remarks: Use $stdout.sync = true in rake tasks to avoid buffering on stdout if task runs concurrently."' 'rails c')
end 		  	    									   			     	   	 						       end                                                  `ruby -e "$SAFE = 3" # Remarks: Use $stdout.sync = true in rake tasks to avoid buffering on stdout if task runs concurrently."' 'rails c')
